Emily runs a dog rescue center and currently has 25 dogs. On Thursday, she received 4 puppies from a nearby town, bringing the total number of dogs to 29. On Friday, a family brought in a stray dog, increasing the count to 30. On Saturday, 2 families adopted 3 dogs each, reducing the total number of dogs to 24. 

*** 

The number of dogs in the rescue center is affected by several factors, including the number of puppies received, stray dogs brought in, and the number of dogs adopted. These events can be represented by placeholders such as [number of puppies received], [number of stray dogs brought in], [number of families who adopted dogs], and [number of dogs adopted per family]. The total number of dogs can be calculated using these placeholders, as shown in the example above.
